draft.title: weather app for runners
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
weather app for runners

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Runners want high contrast, energetic visual feedback to match their workout intensity, even at night
facade.axisTargeted: warm vs cool (cool dominant), calm vs energetic (energetic)
facade.label: Neon glow on dark
facade.acceptImplies: User leans toward high-contrast, stimulating interfaces with bright accent colors on dark backgrounds; may value visibility and performance-readiness over zen calm
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers softer visual language, natural color palettes, or ambient design; may find neon stress-inducing rather than motivating

facade.hypothesis: Runners need to see speed changes instantly reflected in UI motion—fast swipes for fast pace, sluggish for slow—to feel their body's rhythm mirrored back
facade.axisTargeted: Motion responsiveness to user intent: gesture-driven animation vs passive display
facade.label: Velocity feedback
facade.acceptImplies: UI motion should be coupled to runner input speed; transitions feel athletic and reactive
facade.rejectImplies: UI motion should be decoupled from input; transitions feel uniform and meditative regardless of interaction pace

evidence.hypothesis: Runners want high contrast, energetic visual feedback to match their workout intensity, even at night
evidence.implication: User leans toward high-contrast, stimulating interfaces with bright accent colors on dark backgrounds; may value visibility and performance-readiness over zen calm

facade.hypothesis: A voice that cheers you on with upbeat language and emojis (e.g., 'You crushed it! 🔥') will feel more engaging and personal than neutral, data-only messaging.
facade.label: Chirpy, motivational copy
facade.acceptImplies: User leans toward a conversational, encouraging tone—the app should celebrate small wins and use casual, energetic language.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers a calm, factual tone focused on data and performance metrics without personality or hype.

facade.hypothesis: Runners want immediate, glanceable metrics (temp, wind speed) over detailed explanations or forecasts
facade.axisTargeted: Dense data (single metrics) vs narrative context (why it matters for running)
facade.label: Big number, small context
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick scans and metric-first design; show prominent numbers, minimal text around them
facade.rejectImplies: User wants story and reasoning; metrics need framing (e.g., 'Too humid to run hard' rather than bare '78% humidity')

facade.hypothesis: Runners prefer a persistent navigation rail on the left (showing weather summary, alerts, route) while the main content scrolls vertically as cards (hourly forecast, conditions, warnings).
facade.axisTargeted: Layout structure: sidebar persistence vs. tab-based navigation; information density and wayfinding during runs.
facade.label: Sidebar with card feed
facade.acceptImplies: User leans toward always-visible context + scrollable primary content; values quick access to nav without re-tapping; likely values glanceability.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer tab-based flat navigation, full-screen focused views, or horizontal swiping; possibly values immersion or larger content area over persistent chrome.

facade.hypothesis: For a runner app, high-contrast colored sections (weather state, pace, distance) might feel energetic and scannable, or risk appearing overwhelming and arcade-like.
facade.label: Bold color blocks
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers vibrant, high-energy visual language—likely young, performance-focused audience; supports bold CTAs and clear data hierarchy through color.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers restrained, muted, or monochromatic palettes—values calm focus over excitement; app should use subtle gradients, single-color systems, or neutral tones even for key metrics.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
Whether user wants quick-glance widgets vs. deep-dive menu navigation for weather info during runs
Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
Whether user wants energizing, action-oriented feel vs. calm, meditative atmosphere
Whether user prioritizes simple essentials (temp, rain) vs. detailed metrics (humidity, wind, UV)
axes.poleA: Just the basics or all the data

axes.label: Whether user prefers smooth, flowing transitions vs. snappy, instant feedback

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants quick-glance widgets vs. deep-dive menu navigation for weather info during runs
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants energizing, action-oriented feel vs. calm, meditative atmosphere
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prioritizes simple essentials (temp, rain) vs. detailed metrics (humidity, wind, UV)
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers smooth, flowing transitions vs. snappy, instant feedback
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Runners respond better to calming, organic transitions in visual hierarchy than stark contrast; cool-toned gradients reduce cognitive load during high-exertion moments and feel more natural/outdoor-like
facade.axisTargeted: warm vs cool + organic vs digital
facade.label: Soft gradient sky
facade.acceptImplies: User values visual rest and natural aesthetics even during intense activity; may prefer subtle information hierarchy and atmospheric design over bold performance cues
facade.rejectImplies: User confirms preference for crisp, high-contrast, digital-feeling interfaces that signal responsiveness and energy; rejects soft/ambient aesthetics as potentially vague or low-confidence

draft.title: RunWeather
draft.summary: A weather app designed for runners, showing real-time conditions, wind impact, and run-friendly forecasts at a glance.
